  • THReshold EStimation Helping Optimise Local Decisions in antibiotic Stewardship

About Us

The THRESHOLDS Study Group is a non-profit research group which does not receive funding from for-profit or other private companies. The project is supported by the respective participating hospitals and, eventually, requesting grants from public sources of research funding. The THRESHOLDS Study Group is open to collaboration with any hospital or university or health entity interested in its scientific approach, in order to increase scientific knowledge about bacterial resistance and its determinants

Read More

Several posters on antibiotic thresholds were presented at ECCMID conference
10 May, 2014, by Mamoon Al Deyab

Oral and posters presentation at ECCMID Conference, April 2018, Madrid, Spain

Read More

About Us

We aim to detect thresholds for all antibiotics used in a particular hospital in order to establish a policy of use that is not exceeding those thresholds, in the hope that resistance levels would remain at acceptable levels.

Links

  • Aldeyab MA, Al-Hashimy ZS, Al-Yaqoobi M, Conway BR, Darwish Elhajji F, Bond SE, Lattyak EA, Hasan SS, Lattyak WJ. Exploring thresholds and interaction effects among antibiotic usage, covariates, and their effect on antibiotic resistance using an extended-spectrum β-Lactamase-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae case. Expert Rev Anti Infect Ther. 2023;21(7):777-786.
  • Aldeyab MA, Lattyak WJ. Challenges in Forecasting Antimicrobial Resistance. Emerg Infect Dis. 2023;29(7):1496.

COVID-19 pandemic

The COVID-19 pandemic reshaped healthcare, impacting infection control and antibiotic use. It highlighted new challenges in managing antibiotic resistance and raised concerns about long-term effects on antimicrobial practices across the globe
